name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
business: CRISPR Therapeutics is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing CRISPR-based gene-editing therapeutics for serious human diseases, including an approved therapy for sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, and pipeline programs in cardiovascular, autoimmune, oncology, and diabetes.
sellsTo: Patients with severe sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia
Patients with cardiovascular diseases (e.g., hypertriglyceridemia, refractory hypercholesterolemia, elevated Lp(a))
Patients with autoimmune diseases (e.g., systemic lupus erythematosus, systemic sclerosis, inflammatory myositis, immune thrombocytopenia purpura, warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia)
Patients with B-cell malignancies (e.g., large B-cell lymphoma, follicular lymphoma, marginal zone lymphoma, mantle cell lymphoma)
Patients with thromboembolic diseases (e.g., atrial fibrillation, venous thromboembolism)

exposures.sentence: We are a leading biopharmaceutical company focused on the development of CRISPR-based therapeutics, including by using CRISPR/Cas9 technology.

catalysts.event: CASGEVY became first approved CRISPR-based gene-editing therapy
catalysts.sentence: In 2023, CASGEVY became the first-ever approved CRISPR-based gene-editing therapy in the world.

catalysts.event: Partnership with Sirius Therapeutics for siRNA programs
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, we partnered with Sirius and entered into the Sirius Agreement pursuant to which, among other things, we and Sirius will collaborate on the research, development, manufacture, commercialization and use of the Sirius Collaboration Products, including co-development and co-commercialization of CTX611.

catalysts.event: Positive Phase 1 data for CTX310 presented at American Heart Association Scientific Sessions
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we presented positive Phase 1 data from our ongoing clinical trial evaluating CTX310 during a late-breaking session at the American Heart Association Scientific Sessions and published simultaneously in The New England Journal of Medicine.

catalysts.event: Positive data from pivotal studies of CASGEVY in children ages 5-11 presented at ASH
catalysts.sentence: At the American Society of Hematology annual meeting, or ASH, in December 2025, positive data from the pivotal studies of CASGEVY in children ages 5 to 11 years old with SCD or TDT were presented.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable patient population with severe SCD or TDT in US, Canada, Europe and parts of Middle East is approximately 60,000 individuals.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that in the United States, Canada, Europe and parts of the Middle East, the total addressable patient population with severe SCD or TDT is approximately 60,000 individuals.

tamClaims.claim: Worldwide incidence of SCD is 300,000 births annually; 20-25 million people worldwide with disease.
tamClaims.sentence: The worldwide incidence of SCD is estimated to be 300,000 births annually and there are 20-25 million people worldwide with the disease.

tamClaims.claim: Worldwide incidence of beta thalassemia is 60,000 births annually; over 200,000 people alive registered receiving treatment.
tamClaims.sentence: The total worldwide incidence of beta thalassemia is estimated to be 60,000 births annually and there are over 200,000 people worldwide who are alive and registered as receiving treatment for the disease.

tamClaims.claim: Up to 20% of US adults have Lp(a) levels above 50 mg/dL; over 1 million US adults have AVCD.
tamClaims.sentence: Up to 20% of adults in the United States have Lp(a) levels above 50 mg/dL and over 1 million adults in the United States have AVCD.

tamClaims.claim: FCS prevalence 1 in 200,000 to 300,000 in US and EU.
tamClaims.sentence: The prevalence of FCS is 1 in 200,000 to 300,000 individuals in the United States and EU.
MCS has a prevalence of 1 in 250 to 600 individuals.
HoFH has a prevalence of 1 in 200,000 to 1,000,000 adults.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 100,000 individuals in US living with AATD.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that approximately 100,000 individuals in the United States are living with AATD.

tamClaims.claim: ITP occurs in approximately 31,000 patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: ITP occurs in approximately 31,000 patients in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: wAIHA annual incidence 1 to 6 per 100,000; prevalence approximately 1 in 8,000 to 12,000 individuals.
tamClaims.sentence: The annual incidence of wAIHA in the United States is estimated at approximately 1 to 6 cases per 100,000 individuals, with a prevalence of approximately 1 in 8,000 to 12,000 individuals.

tamClaims.claim: CVD accounted for nearly 20.5 million deaths globally in 2021.
tamClaims.sentence: Cardiovascular disease, or CVD, is the leading cause of death globally, accounting for close to one third of all deaths, or nearly 20.5 million people, in 2021.
